big_picture: The Andhra Pradesh High Court called non-compliance with judicial orders by government officials shameful and derogatory to the Constitution. The Supreme Court directed the CBI to probe contracts awarded to Arunachal Pradesh Chief Minister Pema Khandu’s family. The Madurai Sessions Court awarded death penalty to all nine police officers convicted in the Sathankulam custodial deaths case. The Delhi High Court restored satirical X accounts ‘Dr Nimo Yadav’ and ‘Nehr who’ blocked by the Centre’s ordinance. Iran warned of devastating retaliation after Trump’s expletive-laden threats over the Strait of Hormuz. The Election Commission sought legal opinion on BJP candidate PK Krishnadas’ love jihad remark in Kerala. The Internet Freedom Foundation criticized India’s IT rules, stating they have made the internet less free. Five youths were accused of sexually harassing a Japanese tourist near Jaipur’s Jaigarh Fort, with an FIR registered but no arrests made.

story_clusters.headline: Why are gold and silver prices not rising despite the West Asia war?
story_clusters.summary: Gold and silver prices have not risen despite the West Asia war, according to a Business Standard report. Meanwhile, Moody’s reduced India’s FY27 GDP growth forecast to 6% amid the conflict, and Govinda Rao warned that West Asia tensions could increase fiscal risks. An inter-ministerial briefing also addressed recent developments in the region.

The transit was described as an "extraordinary situation" by the Shipping Corporation of India (SCI) chairman.

Finance.summary:
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) cancelled the licence of The Shirpur Merchants’ Co-operative Bank Ltd., Shirpur under Section 22 read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, ceasing its banking operations.

The RBI issued a Master Direction on April 01, 2026, outlining the Facility for Exchange of Notes and Coins under Section 35A of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, addressed to all banks.

Business Standard reports that the RBI may maintain the current repo rate and monetary policy stance.

Legal.summary:
The Andhra Pradesh High Court stated that non-compliance of judicial orders by government officials is shameful and derogatory to the Constitution.

The Supreme Court refused to interfere with the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) order allowing Adani to acquire Jaypee Group’s assets, directing the tribunal to expedite a petition by Vedanta Limited.

The Supreme Court, in a live hearing before a nine-judge bench, announced it would examine pleas on religious discrimination against women and the scope of judicial review under Article 25 of the Constitution.
Legal.top_article_title: Non-Compliance Of Judicial Orders By Govt Officials 'Shameful', Derogatory To Constitution: Andhra Pradesh High Court

Governance.summary: Ahead of poll day, the Election Commission issued a directive requiring individuals or contesting candidates to apply for certification of political advertisements with the District Media Certification and Monitoring Committee. Indians in the US reported a surge in cyber extortion and scams, with complaints rising from 8 to 613 in one year. The Shatabdi Mahotsav opened at AU with enthusiastic participation.
Governance.top_article_title: Ahead of poll day, Election Commission issues directive on political advertisements

Defence.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh laid the foundation stones for three new medical centres and new infrastructure at Base Hospital in Delhi Cantonment to enhance tertiary care for the Armed Forces Medical Services. Young Britons are increasingly enlisting in the military due to job shortages, with the British government leveraging this trend to address both youth unemployment and military recruitment gaps. Nigeria and the US are strengthening military cooperation to counter rising instability in the Sahel region.
Defence.top_article_title: Rajnath lays foundation stones for 3 new centres at R&R, new infra at Base Hospital

Energy.summary:
An Indian-flagged tanker owned by a Japanese shipping firm’s subsidiary passed through the Strait of Hormuz en route to India amid escalating tensions.

Moody’s reduced India’s FY27 GDP growth estimate to 6% due to the West Asia conflict, which supplies over 55% of India’s crude oil imports and over 90% of its LPG.

India confirmed it continues importing energy from Iran despite reports of cargo diversion over payment issues.
Energy.top_article_title: Middle East crisis live: Iran warns of ‘devastating’ retaliation after Trump’s expletive-laden threats over strait of Hormuz

Foreign.summary:
Indian Envoy Meets Nepal’s New Foreign Minister To Discuss Bilateral Ties, with both sides reviewing Nepal-India relations and cooperation.

Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel emphasized a ‘nation first’ ideology at a foundation day event while urging measured responses to rumors amid West Asia tensions.

Bangladesh’s Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman arrived in Delhi for talks with Indian officials.
Foreign.top_article_title: Indian Envoy Meets Nepal’s New Foreign Minister To Discuss Bilateral Ties

Trade.summary: The Confederation of Indian Industry proposed a 20-point policy action plan for the Finance Ministry and Reserve Bank of India, including a temporary three-month moratorium and restructuring window for MSMEs, particularly exporters and units linked to export supply chains. The Global Trade Research Initiative reported that India is largely shielded from new U.S. tariffs as President Trump’s executive order imposing a 100% duty on certain patented pharmaceuticals and ingredients does not significantly impact the country. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade conducted a special drive to expedite the issuance of Export Obligation Discharge Certificates, resulting in a sharp increase in approvals and faster processing rates.
Trade.top_article_title: CII proposes 20 points policy action for Finmin, RBI to face war challenges

Welfare.summary: The BJP’s manifesto promises the Christian community in Kerala 'micro-minority status' and 'equitable access to welfare schemes, scholarships'. The BJP’s welfare schemes remain popular in Assam despite delayed justice for singer Zubeen Garg’s death. In Upper Assam, the BJP faces an energized Opposition campaign led by Gaurav, Akhil, and Lurunjyoti Gogois, though the ruling party retains an advantage.
Welfare.top_article_title: How Christians are responding to BJP’s pre-poll outreach in Kerala

Technology.summary:
The Finance Ministry cleared over ₹1 lakh crore for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, with Cabinet approval expected by April-end.

AI is enhancing legal services by improving efficiency and accuracy in research, document analysis, and case predictions.

India has two sovereign AI programmes—one officially launched and the other operating independently.
Technology.top_article_title: Finance Ministry clears over ₹1 lakh cr for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0; Cabinet nod likely soon

Education.summary:
The Meghalaya Board of School Education will declare the MBOSE Class 10 Result 2026 at 11 am today, with students able to check their results on the official website at mbose.in.

Odisha raised Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe quotas and introduced Other Backward Classes reservation in medical and technical education across all universities, affiliated colleges, Industrial Training Institutes, and polytechnics.

The J&K Assembly cleared a Bill to set up private universities, with Omar Abdullah calling it a ‘milestone’ for youth.
Education.top_article_title: Meghalaya Board MBOSE SSLC result 2026 LIVE: Class 10 results to be declared at 11 am at mbose.in

Health.summary:
Why India’s diabetes epidemic is a policy failure, with stagnating wages, decreasing food quality, and a focus on low-cost carbohydrates making basic nutrition unaffordable.

The mother of the RG Kar Medical College rape and murder victim has joined the BJP and will contest the West Bengal Assembly elections, citing her fight for justice.

AIIMS-B is collaborating with Dutch experts to introduce personalised medications for patients.
Health.top_article_title: Why India’s diabetes epidemic is a policy failure

Infrastructure.summary:
IRFC sanctioned a ₹1,000 crore term loan to Maharashtra State Power Generation Company (MAHAGENCO), its first financing deal of FY27, to support power capacity expansion in Maharashtra.

The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) aims to reduce its debt to below ₹1.5 trillion in FY27 and achieve zero debt by 2030.

The Puri airport project received Stage-I forest clearance from the Centre to enhance global pilgrim connectivity.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: IRFC sanctions ₹1,000 crore loan to Maharashtra power utility in first FY27 deal

Labour.summary: The new Labour Codes are prompting buyers to reassess financials and negotiate safeguards like indemnities and escrows due to increased wage obligations and social security coverage reducing profitability and valuation adjustments. The All India Bank Employees' Association has urged Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman to order a CBI probe into former HDFC Bank Chairman Atanu Chakraborty’s resignation, citing ethical concerns over certain practices. Salaries have been rejigged from April 1 under the new Wage Code, with the restructuring expected to cause a short-term pinch for long-term gains.
Labour.top_article_title: Impact of New Labour Codes on Deals and M & A Transactions

TRAI releases Telecom Subscription Data as on February 2026 shows the latest telecom subscriber statistics for India.

India's telecom subscriber base hits 1.32 billion as broadband adoption surges in February, with the sector gaining 7.31 million subscribers in the month.

Airtel deploys over 4,300 new 5G sites in eastern UP to strengthen network presence across 48 districts in the UP East circle.
Telecom.top_article_title: TRAI releases Telecom Subscription Data as on February 2026

Fintech.summary: The IMF highlighted that tokenization could enhance financial inclusion and efficiency in emerging economies while also posing risks such as regulatory challenges and market volatility.
Fintech.top_article_title: IMF Flags Opportunities and Risks of Tokenization for Emerging Economies

Agriculture.summary:
India's dairy sector leaders in New Delhi emphasized the need for a climate-resilient ecosystem with integrated value chains, innovative financing, and farmer-centric solutions to secure sustainable rural livelihoods.

Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath stated that the farmer registry will ensure transparent and time-bound delivery of government scheme benefits to farmers.

Agrivartan FPO, supported by the SMART Project and Palladium, exported 20 tonnes of bananas from Maharashtra to Dubai, marking a milestone in integrating smallholder farmers into global agri-export markets.
Agriculture.top_article_title: Building a climate-resilient and future-ready dairy sector need of the hour

Urban.summary: A sudden 15-minute rain in Pune caused severe waterlogging and traffic chaos, with experts blaming poor drainage and unusual weather while the civic body held a review meeting amid criticism. The expanded benami provisions now expose informal family property arrangements in India to confiscation and penalties, with courts emphasizing strict compliance and proof of genuine ownership to avoid adverse action. Top realty players in India experienced mixed Q4 results due to launch delays and softer market sentiment.
Urban.top_article_title: How can a hill city be flooded? Rains raise questions over Pune's urban planning

Environment.summary:
53 gharials were released into the river at Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh as part of India’s conservation efforts for the species.

Governments remain divided over the timeline for the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change’s AR7 report, leaving the process in deadlock.

Disagreements between nations over the next IPCC reports highlight the ongoing challenges in the assessment cycle.

DeepTech.summary:
Happiest Minds Technologies is preparing to launch an agentic service delivery platform as its first AI offering, developed over 18 months and positioned as the company’s key differentiator.

The Gujarat High Court has introduced an AI policy that restricts its use in court decisions, reasoning, and orders while allowing limited administrative applications, emphasizing human accountability and data protection.

Razorpay has partnered with OpenAI to enable developers to collect payments via its platform within Codex and ChatGPT workflows in India.
DeepTech.top_article_title: Happiest Minds may launch its first Agentic AI for engineering soon

Data Privacy.summary: The USTR warned that rules under India’s Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act may affect the operations of US credit bureaus in India. Cross Identity announced it is offering VISHWAAS AI at zero cost as readiness for the DPDP Act lags across India Inc.

articles.summary: The Companies Compliance Facilitation Scheme, 2026 (CCFS-2026) allows companies to regularize pending compliance filings at reduced costs, offering penalty relief as a final opportunity. The scheme is aimed at easing compliance burdens for businesses. It is a government initiative to facilitate better regulatory adherence. The analysis highlights its key provisions and implications for corporate compliance.

articles.title: Tax Searches, Digital Data And Privacy Concerns
articles.summary: The Ministry of Finance issued a notification on March 20, 2026, introducing the Income-tax Rules, 2026, which will take effect on April 1, 2026. These rules are framed under the Income-tax Act, 2025, and expand search and seizure powers, particularly in the digital domain. The move raises constitutional questions about potential breaches of the fundamental right to privacy as the state's digital surveillance capabilities grow.
articles.raw_summary: The Ministry of Finance has issued a notification on the Income-tax Rules, 2026 on March 20, 2026. The Rules will come into effect on April 1, 2026. The Rules were framed under the Income-tax Act, 2025, and they implement search and seizure powers provided in Section 247. As we approach a new system, an extension of search and seizure powers in the digital sphere sparks an interesting constitutional issue: do we risk breaching the fundamental right to privacy as a State increasingly enters perso

articles.title: Section 68 Addition Deleted Due to Explained Cash Sales in Books
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that cash deposits during demonetization, backed by recorded sales in books, cannot be taxed under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act. This decision clarifies that explained cash sales in financial records are not subject to unexplained income provisions. The ruling impacts tax assessments for businesses and individuals with cash transactions during the demonetization period. The post was published on TaxGuru.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved cash deposits during demonetization treated as unexplained. The Tribunal held that deposits backed by recorded sales cannot be taxed under Section 68. The post Section 68 Addition Deleted Due to Explained Cash Sales in Books appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Crypto/VDA Taxation: Income-tax Act 1961 vs 2025 Framework
articles.summary: The article compares the existing Income-tax Act, 1961 with the new 2025 tax framework for cryptocurrency and Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs). The key change removes the minimum threshold for Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) on crypto transactions, making even small trades taxable. This introduces stricter compliance requirements with full transaction-level tracking. The analysis highlights the shift toward more rigorous oversight of crypto transactions in India.
articles.raw_summary: The new law removes the minimum threshold for TDS on crypto transactions, making even small trades taxable. articles.summary: The Income-tax Act, 2025 introduces a new law (Section 12AB to RNPO) that shifts charitable entity registration from time-bound approvals to continuous compliance monitoring. The policy replaces previous periodic approval systems with ongoing scrutiny to maintain tax exemptions.

articles.summary: The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act, 2026, passed by the Indian government, decriminalizes minor regulatory violations by replacing criminal penalties with civil fines and adjudication processes across 79 laws and 784 provisions. This reform aims to reduce the burden on courts and simplify compliance for businesses and individuals. The law was enacted to streamline regulatory enforcement while maintaining accountability through civil penalties.

articles.title: Section 69A Addition Deleted Due to Double Taxation of Recorded Sales
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that taxing cash deposits as unexplained income, despite being part of recorded sales, amounts to impermissible double taxation. This decision led to the deletion of Section 69A provisions, which previously allowed such tax treatment. The ruling impacts taxpayers and tax authorities by clarifying that the same income cannot be taxed twice under different provisions.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved treating cash deposits as unexplained despite being part of recorded sales. The Tribunal held that taxing the same income again results in impermissible double taxation. The post Section 69A Addition Deleted Due to Double Taxation of Recorded Sales appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs) under Income-tax Act, 1961
articles.summary: The Income-tax Act, 1961 now mandates a flat 30% tax on transfers of Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs) without allowing deductions except the cost of acquisition. This strict computation rule limits tax benefits and increases the effective tax liability for VDA transactions. The provision applies to all VDA transfers in India, impacting investors, traders, and businesses in the digital asset space.
articles.raw_summary: The provisions mandate taxation of VDA transfers at a flat 30% rate without allowing deductions except cost of acquisition. The key takeaway is that strict computation rules limit tax benefits and increase effective tax liability. articles.title: Gujarat HC issues policy on use of AI in Judicial and Court administration
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court has issued a policy regulating the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in judicial and court administration. The policy restricts AI to administrative and research tasks, explicitly prohibiting its use in judicial decisions or legal reasoning. Judges remain solely responsible for all decisions to uphold fairness and constitutional values. This marks a formal policy directive by a state judiciary on emerging technology in the legal system.
articles.raw_summary: The policy limits AI to administrative and research roles while prohibiting its use in judgments or legal reasoning. It reinforces that judges must retain full responsibility for decisions to preserve fairness and constitutional values. This trend underscores the urgent need for climate action, including policy measures to address ocean warming and its impacts.

articles.title: Succession to office of Sajjadanashin governed by custom and valid nomination, not mere lineal inheritance: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the succession to the office of Sajjadanashin, the spiritual head of a shrine, is governed by custom and valid nomination rather than mere lineal inheritance. The Court emphasized that the Sajjadanashin is not just an administrative manager but primarily responsible for preserving spiritual traditions, guiding disciples, and conducting religious ceremonies. This ruling clarifies the legal framework for succession in such religious institutions.
articles.raw_summary: The Sajjadanashin is not merely an administrative manager of Wakf property but is primarily the spiritual head of the shrine, responsible for preserving the spiritual lineage (silsila), guiding disciples (murids), conducting religious ceremonies such as Urs and Sandal, and maintaining the spiritual traditions associated with the shrine. articles.title: SC Slams Bengal Administration Over Judicial Officers Hostage Crisis in Malda
articles.summary: The Supreme Court criticized the West Bengal administration for failing to protect judicial officers who were taken hostage in Malda, calling it a complete breakdown of law and order. The Court ordered an investigation and stressed that state authorities must be held accountable for their failure to ensure safety. The incident highlights concerns over governance and judicial security in the state.
articles.raw_summary: The Court examined the hostage-taking of judicial officers and held it reflected a complete breakdown of law and order. It ordered a probe and emphasized accountability of state authorities for failing to ensure their safety. articles.title: Clubbing of Income under Income Tax Act, 1961 (Sections 60 to 64)
articles.summary: The article explains provisions under India's Income Tax Act, 1961 (Sections 60 to 64) that prevent tax avoidance by ensuring income transferred indirectly without transferring the underlying asset remains taxable in the transferor's hands. These rules aim to close loopholes where individuals might try to reduce their tax liability by shifting income to others. The provisions are part of India's tax policy framework to maintain fairness in income taxation.
articles.raw_summary: Income transferred without transferring the underlying asset remains taxable in the hands of the transferor. The provisions ensure that tax liability cannot be avoided through indirect income transfers. The post Clubbing of Income under Income Tax Act, 1961 (Sections 60 to 64) appeared first on TaxGuru .

The case highlights debates over judicial accountability and educational content.
articles.raw_summary: In separate affidavits, academics told SC that the idea behind including chapter on judiciary in Class 8 NCERT social science textbook was to engage students in critical thinking, it is learnt.

articles.title: Exclusive: GST on ride-hailing platforms faces fresh scrutiny as industry flags SaaS model mismatch
articles.summary: Industry stakeholders have raised concerns about India's current GST framework for ride-hailing platforms, arguing that the 5% tax on fares collected by e-commerce operators does not adequately differentiate between traditional cab aggregators and SaaS-based platforms. They claim this mismatch creates compliance difficulties, increases costs, and creates regulatory uncertainty. The issue involves ride-hailing companies, industry bodies, and tax authorities.

The verdict highlights issues of police brutality and the need for accountability in law enforcement.

articles.title: Wrong Section, Wrong Valuation – ITAT Quashes Capital Gains Assessment
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) overturned a capital gains assessment because the assessing officer (AO) incorrectly used Section 142A instead of Section 55A for valuation. The tribunal ruled that this procedural error invalidated the assessment. The case highlights the importance of correct legal provisions in tax adjudication. The ruling impacts how future capital gains assessments are conducted by tax authorities.
articles.raw_summary: The dispute involved incorrect invocation of valuation provisions by the AO. The Tribunal ruled that using Section 142A instead of 55A vitiated the assessment. The post Wrong Section, Wrong Valuation   ITAT Quashes Capital Gains Assessment appeared first on TaxGuru .

The move highlights China's efforts to bypass global containment and strengthen its own tech capabilities.

articles.title: Homebuyers Cannot Claim ITC Benefit in Fully Post-GST Projects: GSTAT
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) ruled that homebuyers cannot claim Input Tax Credit (ITC) benefits in projects fully governed under the GST regime. The tribunal held that since construction, agreements, and payments occurred entirely post-GST, prices already included ITC, and no additional benefit needed to be passed on. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of real estate projects under the GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: GSTAT held that no anti-profiteering violation arises where construction, agreement, and payments occur entirely in the GST regime. Since prices already factored ITC, no benefit was required to be passed on. The post Homebuyers Cannot Claim ITC Benefit in Fully Post-GST Projects: GSTAT appeared first on TaxGuru .

Raising fuel prices can help manage the fiscal deficit.

articles.title: Nvidia acquisition of SchedMD sparks worry among AI specialists about software access
articles.summary: Nvidia announced its acquisition of SchedMD, the company behind Slurm, an open-source software used to schedule computing tasks. Slurm is essential for training large language models like those powering AI chatbots and is also used on government supercomputers for tasks such as weather forecasting and nuclear research. The deal has raised concerns among AI specialists about potential restrictions on software access, which could impact AI development and critical infrastructure.

articles.title: Tax on Winnings From Online Games: Section 115BBJ of Income-tax Act, 1961
articles.summary: The Income-tax Act, 1961, under Section 115BBJ, imposes a uniform 30% tax on income from online gaming winnings, regardless of whether the game involves skill or chance. articles.title: Even Property Owner Can Be Guilty of Trespass: Kerala HC Explains Why
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court ruled that even a property owner can be guilty of trespass if they unlawfully enter premises in the lawful possession of another with criminal intent. The court clarified that offenses like criminal trespass are against possession, not ownership. This ruling underscores that ownership rights do not permit illegal entry into another's lawful possession.
articles.raw_summary: Offences such as criminal trespass and house trespass are offences against possession and not against ownership. Therefore, even a true owner cannot, under the guise of ownership, unlawfully enter premises in the lawful possession of another with the intent to commit an offence.  The post Even Property Owner Can Be Guilty of Trespass: Kerala HC Explains Why appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.summary: The article analyzes India's Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), noting its failure to resolve cases within expected timelines and achieve higher recovery rates (limited to 30%). It discusses the proposed IBC Amendment Bill 2025, which aims to expedite processes and prevent misuse, but emphasizes the need for practical improvements. The analysis is based on the performance of the existing legal framework and potential reforms.

articles.summary: NeSL (National E-Governance Services Limited) is now providing instant access to digital default records for insolvency cases in India, enabling authorities to quickly verify debt and claims. This follows amendments to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code that designate NeSL as the central gateway for all insolvency proceedings. NeSL is also developing electronic insurance surety bonds to support MSMEs and aims to build digital infrastructure for high-trust transactions.

NeSL aims to build digital infrastructure for high-trust transactions.

The program is being implemented in collaboration with local authorities to support tribal farmers.

The industry seeks policy relief to stabilize prices and ensure supply continuity.

articles.title: US Allies Work On Plan B For Hormuz Strait If Trump Walks Away
articles.summary: More than 40 US allies, including key partners, met to discuss contingency plans for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The move signals deep concern over the waterway's crisis, triggered by US-Iran tensions under President Trump's policies. The Strait is a critical global oil transit route, and any disruption could impact energy supplies. India, heavily reliant on oil imports, may face indirect consequences from such geopolitical developments.

articles.title: Mumbai’s 113-year-old Elphinstone bridge demolished, marks end of colonial-era landmark
articles.summary: Mumbai has bid farewell to the 113-year-old Elphinstone Road Overbridge, a colonial-era structure that shaped commuter movement for over a century. Its demolition clears space for new suburban rail lines and a planned double-decker corridor, marking a significant step in the city’s infrastructure upgrade.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz key to market direction amid ceasefire talks: Politeia Research
articles.summary: Sanjay Pulipaka of Politeia Research Foundation highlighted that the Strait of Hormuz is a major sticking point in ceasefire talks. Iran has indicated it will not reopen the strait unless hostilities fully cease, making it a key bargaining tool. The strait is vital for global oil supply, and its closure could disrupt energy markets, impacting India's oil imports and economic stability.

Iran has indicated reluctance to reopen the strait unless there is a complete halt in hostilities, making it a major bargaining tool.

Sarma accuses Cong of using Pak-linked group’s docs to forge his wife’s passports
articles.summary: Congress alleged that Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma’s wife holds passports from the UAE, Egypt, and Antigua and Barbuda, and owns property in Dubai. Sarma countered by accusing Congress of using documents linked to a Pakistan-based group to forge these passports. The allegations raise questions about passport verification processes and foreign asset ownership rules in India. No official policy action has been announced yet, but the claims could prompt regulatory or legal scrutiny.

articles.title: Trump administration proposes expanding Chinese tech gear crackdown
articles.summary: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) in 2021 added Huawei, ZTE, Hytera, Hikvision, and Dahua to its 'Covered List' of companies deemed a national security risk. The Trump administration now proposes expanding this crackdown on Chinese tech gear. This move could influence global supply chains and regulatory approaches to foreign technology, including in India's telecom and surveillance sectors.

articles.summary: Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw announced key railway infrastructure projects for FY2025-26, including the development of 119 Amrit Bharat stations and an all-weather rail link to Kashmir. articles.title: India services growth slows to 14-month low in March amid rising costs, weak demand
articles.summary: India's services sector saw its slowest growth in 14 months during March. This slowdown was influenced by the Middle East conflict affecting domestic demand. However, overseas orders reached near record levels. Input costs intensified significantly. Despite these challenges, employment expanded, and business confidence reached a 12-year high. The overall economic expansion slowed.

The overall economic expansion slowed.

articles.title: Gold imports rise by nearly 29% to $69 bn in Apr-Feb 2025-26
articles.summary: India's gold imports saw a significant rise of 28.73 percent, reaching USD 69 billion in the first eleven months of fiscal 2025-26. The increased gold imports also impact the country's current account deficit.

The strategists emphasize portfolio adjustments to mitigate risks from volatile energy markets.

articles.title: US crude on Thursday did something it's never done before as Iran war continues
articles.summary: US crude oil prices on Thursday experienced an unprecedented market movement amid the ongoing Iran war. The situation stems from perceptions that US President Donald Trump may intervene to end the conflict. The development is significant for global oil markets, which India relies on for energy imports, potentially affecting fuel prices and economic stability in the country.
articles.raw_summary: The market has had the perception that President Donald Trump will quickly end the war.

articles.title: Trump's threats could disrupt global oil supply chains, warns Geosphere Capital's Arvind Sanger
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to target energy infrastructure if no deal is reached, raising concerns about disruptions to global oil supply chains, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz. Analysts at Geosphere Capital warn that the conflict lacks a clear resolution, increasing the risk of prolonged impacts on oil markets. Rising crude prices reflect these supply risks. The situation could have implications for India's energy security and policy responses.
articles.raw_summary: Rising crude prices reflect concerns over supply risks, especially around the Strait of Hormuz. US President Donald Trump has signalled possible escalation targeting energy infrastructure if no deal is reached. Analysts say the conflict lacks a clear end point, raising the risk of prolonged impact on global oil supply and markets.

articles.title: Will crude oil prices tank? Someone has bet nearly $1 billion on this via leverage
articles.summary: Investors have placed nearly $1 billion in leveraged bets on a potential decline in crude oil prices. The United States Oil Fund (USO) and United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO) saw record inflows of $700 million and $600 million respectively in March, the highest since the pandemic. These movements reflect market sentiment and could impact India's energy costs and inflation, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: Its not just the bearish bets, even the bullish funds have drawn record money. The United States Oil Fund (USO) attracted flows worth nearly $700 million in March, the most since the pandemic, while the United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO) saw flows worth a record $600 million.

The cell claims a 100% success rate in removing problematic content. This initiative raises questions about digital surveillance, free speech, and law enforcement's role in regulating online content during elections.
articles.raw_summary: Operational since March 20, the cell has acted on 17 cases covering 27 profiles and handles across platforms, with one FIR registered and the prime accused arrested till March 29. The effective content removal rate stands at 100 per cent.

The report underscores the need for conservation efforts to address environmental and disaster management challenges in the region.

This significant loss, detailed in a CAG report, degraded ecosystems and contributed to the devastating 2014 Kashmir floods by reducing natural flood buffers.

The highway is a vital all-weather route connecting Jammu and Srinagar.

articles.title: Softshell turtle sighted at 2,950 ft in Bageshwar, prompts forest dept survey
articles.summary: A softshell turtle was spotted at an unusually high altitude of 2,950 feet in Bageshwar, Uttarakhand, by the forest department. Divisional Forest Officer Aditya Ratna noted that these turtles are typically found at lower elevations, making this sighting rare. The forest department has initiated a survey to assess the situation and its ecological implications.
articles.raw_summary: Aditya Ratna, divisional forest officer of Bageshwar, said the sighting was unusual because these turtles are typically found at lower altitudes.

articles.title: ‘Your wife has bank links in Pakistan’: Himanta Sarma’s wife to Gaurav Gogoi amid passport, Dubai links row
articles.summary: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife, Riniki Bhuyan, alleged that Gaurav Gogoi's wife has bank links in Pakistan, escalating a row involving passport and Dubai connections. Earlier, Sarma had claimed Gogoi’s wife had multiple bank accounts in Pakistan. The exchange highlights political tensions between leaders from the Congress and BJP.
articles.raw_summary: Riniki Bhuyan's remarks come amid earlier claims by chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma alleging Gaurav Gogoi’s wife had “several” bank accounts in Pakistan.

articles.title: Japanese tourist harassed by five men near Jaigarh fort in Jaipur
articles.summary: A Japanese tourist was harassed by five men near Jaigarh Fort in Jaipur. Police have collected CCTV footage of the suspects walking away from the scene. The incident highlights concerns about safety for foreign visitors and may prompt discussions on security measures or policy responses to such crimes.
articles.raw_summary: The police have collected the CCTV footage of the five men walking down the hill after the incident.

articles.title: Left leans on towering Pinarayi Vijayan in uphill battle to keep Kerala
articles.summary: The CPI(M) leader Pinarayi Vijayan is seeking a third consecutive term as Kerala's Chief Minister, a feat no politician in the state has achieved since its formation. The Left parties are rallying behind him, emphasizing there is no alternative to his leadership. This election could shape the state's governance and policy priorities in the coming years.
articles.raw_summary: There is no alternative to Vijayan, the CPI(M) leader seeking a third consecutive term, a feat no politician in Kerala has achieved since the state was formed.

articles.title: Left leans on towering Vijayan in uphill battle to keep Kerala
articles.summary: At 81, Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan is leading the Left Democratic Front (LDF) in a challenging election battle, emphasizing his government's focus on development ('vikasanam'). The article highlights the political dynamics in Kerala, where Vijayan's leadership is central to the LDF's efforts to retain power. The outcome of this election could shape the state's policy priorities, particularly around governance and development initiatives.
articles.raw_summary: At 81, Vijayan is chasing history; and his message is “vikasanam” (development).

articles.title: Industry bodies warn of migrant labour exodus
articles.summary: Industry associations have warned that cooking fuel shortages, exacerbated by the West Asia conflict, could lead to a migrant labour exodus and increased stress in micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs). The shortage of cooking fuel may disrupt the workforce, particularly migrant labourers, and impact businesses already facing economic challenges. Industry bodies have raised concerns about the potential fallout, which could necessitate policy interventions to address labour welfare and ene
articles.raw_summary: Industry associations warn of rising stress in MSMEs and potential migrant labor exodus due to cooking fuel shortages linked to the West Asia conflict.

articles.title: Iran mocks Trump with 'we've lost the keys' jibe amid Strait of Hormuz standoff
articles.summary: Iran mocked U.S. President Donald Trump after he issued an ultimatum threatening devastating attacks if Iran did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The standoff involves threats over a key oil shipping route, raising concerns about global oil supply disruptions. India, heavily reliant on oil imports, could be indirectly affected by such geopolitical tensions.
articles.raw_summary: Iran's jibe come after Trump issued a 'f*cking ultimatum' demanding that Tehran reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face devastating attacks on its infrastructure.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Why Opec+'s output increase in May won't cool global oil prices
articles.summary: Despite OPEC+'s move to raise output and IEA's emergency release, global crude oil prices are unlikely to fall significantly. The ongoing war in Iran, blocked shipping lanes and attacks on energy infrastructure keep supply tight.

articles.title: ETtech Explainer: Agentic harness, the software that makes AI tick
articles.summary: Indian enterprises are increasingly adopting AI agents for complex business workflows, but the crucial 'agentic harness' is emerging as the key differentiator. This software framework enables AI models to act autonomously and safely, managing memory, tools, and human oversight, becoming the most contested layer in the AI value chain.
